- [CD4-positive exhausted alpha-beta T cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020030) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [human_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#human_subset) 

- [CD4-positive exhausted alpha-beta T cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020030) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [CD4-positive exhausted alpha-beta T cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020030) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A CD4-positive alpha-beta T cell that displays impaired function and altered differentiation as a result of chronic antigenic stimulation (e.g., chronic infection, tumors, or persistent inflammation). This state is characterised by sustained expression of PD-1 as a shared feature. Additional exhaustion-associated molecules, including the transcription factor TOX, and context- or stage-dependent changes in markers such as LAG-3, TIM-3, CD39, and T-bet, may also be observed; however, the expression levels of these molecules vary depending on exhaustion stage and tissue environment." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:31390978" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:36907685" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:38166256" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:31207603" 

- [CD4-positive exhausted alpha-beta T cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020030) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "CD4-positive exhausted alpha-beta T cell" 

- [CD4-positive exhausted alpha-beta T cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020030) EquivalentTo [CD4-positive, alpha-beta T cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0000624) and ([has plasma membrane part](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002104) some [programmed cell death protein 1](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PR_000001919)) 

- [CD4-positive exhausted alpha-beta T cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020030) SubClassOf [exhausted T cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0011025) 

- [CD4-positive exhausted alpha-beta T cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020030) SubClassOf [has plasma membrane part](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002104) some [programmed cell death protein 1](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PR_000001919) 

- [CD4-positive exhausted alpha-beta T cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020030) SubClassOf [CD4-positive, alpha-beta T cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0000624) 
  - [is_inferred](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#is_inferred) "true"

- [CD8-positive exhausted alpha-beta T cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020031) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [human_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#human_subset) 

- [CD8-positive exhausted alpha-beta T cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020031) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [CD8-positive exhausted alpha-beta T cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020031) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A CD8-positive alpha-beta T cell that displays impaired function and altered differentiation as a result of chronic antigenic stimulation (e.g., chronic infection, tumours, or persistent inflammation). This state is characterised by sustained expression of PD-1 as a shared feature. Additional exhaustion-associated molecules, including expression of the transcription factor TOX and reduced T-bet, and context- or stage-dependent changes in markers such as LAG-3, TIM-3, CD39, and EOMES, may also be observed; however, the expression levels of these molecules vary depending on exhaustion stage and disease context." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:36907685" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:38166256" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:31207603" 

- [CD8-positive exhausted alpha-beta T cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020031) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "CD8-positive exhausted alpha-beta T cell" 

- [CD8-positive exhausted alpha-beta T cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020031) EquivalentTo [CD8-positive, alpha-beta T cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0000625) and ([has plasma membrane part](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002104) some [programmed cell death protein 1](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PR_000001919)) 

- [CD8-positive exhausted alpha-beta T cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020031) SubClassOf [exhausted T cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0011025) 

- [CD8-positive exhausted alpha-beta T cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020031) SubClassOf [has plasma membrane part](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002104) some [programmed cell death protein 1](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PR_000001919) 

- [CD8-positive exhausted alpha-beta T cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020031) SubClassOf [CD8-positive, alpha-beta T cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0000625) 
  - [is_inferred](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#is_inferred) "true"

- [CD8-positive, alpha-beta memory T cell, CD45RO-positive](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0001203) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A CD8-positive, alpha-beta T cell with memory phenotype indicated by being CD45RO and CD127-positive. ### Dogiel type II neuron `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020045`

#### Added
- Class: [Dogiel type II neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020045) 

- [Dogiel type II neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020045) [term tracker item](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000233) [3471](https://github.com/obophenotype/cell-ontology/issues/3471) 

- [Dogiel type II neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020045) [creator](http://purl.org/dc/terms/creator) "GitHub Copilot" 

- [Dogiel type II neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020045) [date](http://purl.org/dc/terms/date) "2026-03-10T14:42:58Z"^^[dateTime](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime) 

- [Dogiel type II neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020045) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [human_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#human_subset) 

- [Dogiel type II neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020045) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [Dogiel type II neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020045) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A neuron characterised by Dogiel type II morphology: a large, smooth, oval soma bearing multiple long axon-like processes (multiaxonal) that extend without branching until they reach their targets. The soma lacks the short lamellar or spiny dendrites characteristic of Dogiel type I neurons. Dogiel type II neurons were first described by Alexander Dogiel in 1899 based on methylene blue staining in gastrointestinal ganglia. In the enteric nervous system, Dogiel type II neurons correspond to intrinsic primary afferent neurons (IPANs) and exhibit AH-type electrophysiology (prolonged afterhyperpolarization following an action potential)." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:34170401" 

- [Dogiel type II neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020045) [has exact synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasExactSynonym) "multiaxonal enteric neuron" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:34170401" 

- [Dogiel type II neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020045) [has exact synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasExactSynonym) "type II enteric neuron" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:34170401" 

- [Dogiel type II neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020045) [has_related_synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasRelatedSynonym) "AH neuron" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:34170401" 

  - [has_synonym_type](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasSynonymType) [abbreviation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0003000) 

- [Dogiel type II neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020045) [has_related_synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasRelatedSynonym) "Dogiel II neuron" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:34170401" 

  - [has_synonym_type](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasSynonymType) [abbreviation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0003000) 

- [Dogiel type II neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020045) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "Dogiel type II neuron" 

- [Dogiel type II neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020045) SubClassOf [enteric neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0007011) 


### Dogiel type II neuron of myenteric plexus `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020054`

#### Added
- Class: [Dogiel type II neuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020054)

- [Dogiel type II neuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020054) [creator](http://purl.org/dc/terms/creator) "GitHub Copilot" 

- [Dogiel type II neuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020054) [date](http://purl.org/dc/terms/date) "2026-03-10T14:42:58Z"^^[dateTime](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime) 

- [Dogiel type II neuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020054) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [human_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#human_subset) 

- [Dogiel type II neuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020054) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [Dogiel type II neuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020054) [has exact synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasExactSynonym) "AH-type myenteric neuron" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:34170401" 

- [Dogiel type II neuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020054) [has exact synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasExactSynonym) "multiaxonal myenteric sensory neuron" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:34170401" 

- [Dogiel type II neuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020054) [has exact synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasExactSynonym) "type II myenteric neuron" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:34170401" 

- [Dogiel type II neuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020054) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "An intrinsic primary afferent neuron of the myenteric plexus characterised by Dogiel type II morphology: a large, smooth, oval soma bearing multiple long axon-like processes that extend without branching until they reach their targets in both the myenteric and submucosal plexuses and the mucosa. The soma lacks the dendrites characteristic of Dogiel type I neurons and is larger in cross-sectional area than either motor neuron type. This neuron is immunopositive for choline acetyltransferase (ChAT) and immunonegative for neuronal nitric oxide synthase (NOS1). It exhibits AH-type electrophysiology, characterised by a prolonged afterhyperpolarization (AHP) following an action potential. Substance P (encoded by TAC1) expression has been reported in subsets across species." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:40954253" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:34170401" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:37355216" 

- [Dogiel type II neuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020054) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "Dogiel type II neuron of myenteric plexus" 

- [Dogiel type II neuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020054) SubClassOf [Dogiel type II neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020045) 

- [Dogiel type II neuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020054) SubClassOf [intrinsic primary afferent neuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020046)

### NK T cell activation involved in immune response `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0002288`
#### Removed
- [NK T cell activation involved in immune response](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0002288) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The change in morphology and behavior of a mature or immature natural killer T cell resulting from exposure to a mitogen, cytokine, chemokine, cellular ligand, or an antigen for which it is specific, leading to the initiation or perpetuation of an immune response." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:15771592" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:add" 

- [NK T cell activation involved in immune response](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0002288) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "NK T cell activation involved in immune response" 

- [NK T cell activation involved in immune response](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0002288) EquivalentTo [NK T cell activation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0051132) and ([part of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000050) some [immune response](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0006955)) 

- [NK T cell activation involved in immune response](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0002288) SubClassOf [alpha-beta T cell activation involved in immune response](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0002287) 

- [NK T cell activation involved in immune response](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0002288) SubClassOf [NK T cell activation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0051132)

- [ascending interneuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020050) [date](http://purl.org/dc/terms/date) "2026-03-10T14:42:58Z"^^[dateTime](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime)

- [ascending interneuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020050) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [ascending interneuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020050) [has exact synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasExactSynonym) "ascending myenteric interneuron" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:34170401" 

- [ascending interneuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020050) [has exact synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasExactSynonym) "cholinergic enkephalinergic myenteric interneuron" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:34170401" 

- [ascending interneuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020050) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "An interneuron of the myenteric plexus whose axon projects orally (in the ascending direction) along the gut axis. This neuron is immunopositive for choline acetyltransferase (ChAT) and enkephalin (ENK), and forms the excitatory limb of ascending reflex pathways that coordinate peristalsis." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:40954253" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:34170401" 

- [ascending interneuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020050) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "ascending interneuron of myenteric plexus" 

- [ascending interneuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020050) SubClassOf [interneuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020047)

### calcified zone articular chondrocyte `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020040`

#### Added
- Class: [calcified zone articular chondrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020040) 

- [calcified zone articular chondrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020040) [contributor](http://purl.org/dc/terms/contributor) [0000-0003-0098-4399](https://orcid.org/0000-0003-0098-4399) 

- [calcified zone articular chondrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020040) [date](http://purl.org/dc/terms/date) "2026-02-18T11:06:13Z"^^[dateTime](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime) 

- [calcified zone articular chondrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020040) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [human_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#human_subset) 

- [calcified zone articular chondrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020040) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [calcified zone articular chondrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020040) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "An articular chondrocyte located in the calcified cartilage zone below the tidemark at the interface between articular cartilage and subchondral bone, embedded within a mineralized extracellular matrix. In humans, this cell exhibits a hypertrophic-like phenotype and typically expresses collagen type X (COL10A1). Together with the surrounding calcified matrix, it forms a transitional region that mechanically couples the deep articular cartilage to the underlying subchondral bone." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:23015907" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:18455690" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:41226342" 

- [calcified zone articular chondrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020040) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "calcified zone articular chondrocyte" 

- [calcified zone articular chondrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020040) SubClassOf [articular chondrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_1001607) 

- [calcified zone articular chondrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020040) SubClassOf [expresses](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002292) some [collagen alpha-1(X) chain](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PR_000005693)

- [calretinin-negative intrinsic primary afferent neuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020056) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "An intrinsic primary afferent neuron of the myenteric plexus that lacks calretinin expression. This neuron shares the Dogiel type II morphology and AH-type electrophysiology of all myenteric IPANs, and is immunopositive for choline acetyltransferase (ChAT) and immunonegative for neuronal nitric oxide synthase (NOS1)." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:40954253" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:34170401"

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:37355216" 

- [calretinin-negative intrinsic primary afferent neuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020056) [has_related_synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasRelatedSynonym) "SN2"

### cellular anatomical structure `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0110165`
#### Removed
- [cellular anatomical structure](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0110165) SubClassOf [CARO_0000000](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CARO_0000000) 

#### Added
- [cellular anatomical structure](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0110165) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A part of a cellular organism that is either an immaterial entity or a material entity with granularity above the level of a protein complex but below that of an anatomical system. Or, a substance produced by a cellular organism with granularity above the level of a protein complex." 

- [cellular anatomical structure](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0110165) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "Components are larger than individual macromolecular entities. It is tricky to define distinction between 'cell component' and 'macromolecular entity', e.g. ribosome. We would like to exclude most protein complexes."@en 

- [cellular anatomical structure](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0110165) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "Overlaps with some cellular components from GO"@en 

- [cellular anatomical structure](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0110165) SubClassOf [has part](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000051) some [COB_0000013](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/COB_0000013)

### cervical dorsal root ganglion CGRP neuron `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079000`

#### Added
- Class: [cervical dorsal root ganglion CGRP neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079000) 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion CGRP neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079000) [contributor](http://purl.org/dc/terms/contributor) [0000-0001-6677-8489](https://orcid.org/0000-0001-6677-8489) 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion CGRP neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079000) [date](http://purl.org/dc/terms/date) "2026-03-13T11:13:54Z"^^[dateTime](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime) 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion CGRP neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079000) [has_related_synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasRelatedSynonym) "peptidergic nociceptor" 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion CGRP neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079000) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [human_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#human_subset) 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion CGRP neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079000) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion CGRP neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079000) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A peptidergic nociceptor whose soma is located in the cervical dorsal root ganglion, characterized by expression of calcitonin gene-related peptide (CGRP, encoded by CALCA). This small- to medium-diameter neuron belongs to the TrkA-positive peptidergic subpopulation and typically co-expresses substance P. It innervates peripheral tissues including skin and viscera, where it mediates neurogenic inflammation and nociceptive signalling through release of CGRP from peripheral terminals during tissue injury (Haberberger et al. 2019, PMID:31293388). In human DRG, CGRP-immunoreactive neurons represent a substantial proportion of nociceptors, comparable to the pattern observed in rodents (Rostock et al. 2018, PMID:29229553)." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1016/S0304-3940(00)00772-2" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:31293388" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:29229553" 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion CGRP neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079000) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "cervical dorsal root ganglion CGRP neuron" 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion CGRP neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079000) SubClassOf [BFO_0000002](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000002) 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion CGRP neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079000) SubClassOf [sensory neuron of dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_1001451) 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion CGRP neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079000) SubClassOf [has soma location](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002100) some [cervical dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002834) 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion CGRP neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079000) SubClassOf [expresses](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002292) some [calcitonin gene-related peptide 1](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PR_000027226) 


### cervical dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079002`

#### Added
- Class: [cervical dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079002) 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079002) [contributor](http://purl.org/dc/terms/contributor) [0000-0001-6677-8489](https://orcid.org/0000-0001-6677-8489) 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079002) [date](http://purl.org/dc/terms/date) "2026-03-13T11:13:54Z"^^[dateTime](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime) 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079002) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [human_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#human_subset) 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079002) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079002) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A nociceptor whose soma is located in the cervical dorsal root ganglion and that expresses the voltage-gated sodium channel Nav1.7 (encoded by SCN9A). This neuron is characterized by Nav1.7-dependent action potential initiation and amplification, which is essential for normal pain sensation in humans. Nav1.7 contributes approximately 50% of total sodium channel current in human DRG nociceptors (Chang et al. 2018, PMID:28424991). In human DRG, Nav1.7 immunoreactivity is detected in 54-57% of TrkA-positive neurons (Rostock et al. 2018, PMID:29229553). Human genetic evidence confirms Nav1.7 as necessary and sufficient for nociceptive function in these neurons." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1007/s12264-017-0132-3" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:29229553" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1038/nn.3602" 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079002) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "cervical dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron" 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079002) SubClassOf [BFO_0000002](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000002) 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079002) SubClassOf [sensory neuron of dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_1001451) 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079002) SubClassOf [has soma location](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002100) some [cervical dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002834) 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079002) SubClassOf [expresses](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002292) some [sodium channel protein type 9 subunit alpha](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PR_000002104) 


### cervical dorsal root ganglion Nav1.9 neuron `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079003`

#### Added
- Class: [cervical dorsal root ganglion Nav1.9 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079003) 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion Nav1.9 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079003) [contributor](http://purl.org/dc/terms/contributor) [0000-0001-6677-8489](https://orcid.org/0000-0001-6677-8489) 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion Nav1.9 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079003) [date](http://purl.org/dc/terms/date) "2026-03-13T11:13:54Z"^^[dateTime](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime) 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion Nav1.9 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079003) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [human_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#human_subset) 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion Nav1.9 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079003) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion Nav1.9 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079003) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A nociceptor whose soma is located in the cervical dorsal root ganglion and that expresses the voltage-gated sodium channel Nav1.9 (encoded by SCN11A). This neuron is characterized by a persistent, tetrodotoxin-resistant sodium current that modulates resting membrane potential and amplifies subthreshold depolarizations, contributing to enhanced excitability during inflammation (Dib-Hajj et al. 2002, PMID:11972962). In human DRG, Nav1.9-immunoreactive neurons constitute approximately 26% of TrkA-positive neurons, a significantly higher proportion than the 12% observed in mouse (Rostock et al. 2018, PMID:29229553), suggesting species differences in inflammatory pain processing." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:29229553" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1038/nn.3602" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1016/S0166-2236(02)02150-1" 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion Nav1.9 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079003) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "cervical dorsal root ganglion Nav1.9 neuron" 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion Nav1.9 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079003) SubClassOf [BFO_0000002](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000002) 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion Nav1.9 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079003) SubClassOf [sensory neuron of dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_1001451) 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion Nav1.9 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079003) SubClassOf [has soma location](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002100) some [cervical dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002834)

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion Piezo2 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079005) [date](http://purl.org/dc/terms/date) "2026-03-13T11:13:54Z"^^[dateTime](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime)

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion Piezo2 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079005) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A mechanosensitive sensory neuron whose soma is located in the cervical dorsal root ganglion and that expresses the mechanically activated ion channel Piezo2 (encoded by PIEZO2). This neuron transduces mechanical stimuli and is involved in light touch perception and proprioception. In human DRG, Piezo2-immunoreactive neurons constitute approximately 35% of TrkA-positive neurons, compared to 26% in mouse (Rostock et al. 2018, PMID:29229553). This subpopulation includes both low-threshold mechanoreceptors mediating innocuous touch and a subset of mechanically sensitive nociceptors, reflecting the dual role of Piezo2 in somatosensation across species." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:29229553" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1016/j.neuroscience.2017.11.047" 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion Piezo2 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079005) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "cervical dorsal root ganglion Piezo2 neuron" 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion Piezo2 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079005) SubClassOf [BFO_0000002](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000002) 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion Piezo2 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079005) SubClassOf [sensory neuron of dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_1001451) 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion Piezo2 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079005) SubClassOf [has soma location](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002100) some [cervical dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002834) 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion Piezo2 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079005) SubClassOf [expresses](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002292) some [piezo-type mechanosensitive ion channel component 2](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PR_000032708)

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion RET neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079006) [date](http://purl.org/dc/terms/date) "2026-03-13T11:13:54Z"^^[dateTime](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime) 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion RET neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079006) [has_related_synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasRelatedSynonym) "non-peptidergic nociceptor" 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion RET neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079006) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [human_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#human_subset) 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion RET neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079006) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion RET neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079006) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A non-peptidergic nociceptor whose soma is located in the cervical dorsal root ganglion and that expresses the receptor tyrosine kinase RET (encoded by RET). This neuron is distinguished from peptidergic nociceptors by its dependence on glial cell-derived neurotrophic factor family ligands rather than nerve growth factor, and in rodents it characteristically binds isolectin B4. In human DRG, RET-immunoreactive neurons constitute approximately 46% of TrkA-positive neurons, a significantly higher proportion than the 23% observed in mouse (Rostock et al. 2018, PMID:29229553), indicating an expanded non-peptidergic nociceptor compartment in humans." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:29229553" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1016/j.neuroscience.2017.11.047" 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion RET neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079006) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "cervical dorsal root ganglion RET neuron" 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion RET neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079006) SubClassOf [BFO_0000002](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000002) 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion RET neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079006) SubClassOf [sensory neuron of dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_1001451) 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion RET neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079006) SubClassOf [has soma location](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002100) some [cervical dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002834)

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion TRPV1 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079008) [date](http://purl.org/dc/terms/date) "2026-03-13T11:13:54Z"^^[dateTime](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime) 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion TRPV1 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079008) [has_related_synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasRelatedSynonym) "capsaicin-sensitive nociceptor" 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion TRPV1 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079008) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [human_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#human_subset) 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion TRPV1 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079008) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion TRPV1 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079008) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A polymodal nociceptor whose soma is located in the cervical dorsal root ganglion and that expresses the transient receptor potential vanilloid 1 channel (TRPV1, encoded by TRPV1). This neuron detects noxious heat above 43 degrees Celsius, protons, and endogenous lipid mediators, and is activated by capsaicin. In human DRG, TRPV1-immunoreactive neurons constitute approximately 54% of TrkA-positive neurons, significantly higher than the 35% observed in mouse (Rostock et al. 2018, PMID:29229553). These neurons are predominantly small-diameter nociceptors that innervate skin and visceral organs, where they function as primary detectors of thermal and chemical noxious stimuli." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1007/s12264-017-0132-3" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:29229553" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1016/j.neuroscience.2017.11.047" 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion TRPV1 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079008) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "cervical dorsal root ganglion TRPV1 neuron" 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion TRPV1 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079008) SubClassOf [BFO_0000002](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000002) 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion TRPV1 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079008) SubClassOf [sensory neuron of dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_1001451)

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion endothelin-1 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079001) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A sensory neuron whose soma is located in the cervical dorsal root ganglion and that expresses endothelin-1 (EDN1). In rat DRG, EDN1 immunoreactivity has been detected in a subset of neuron somata (Giaid et al. 1989, PMID:2678100). Endothelin-1 acts as an endogenous pain mediator, sensitizing nociceptors through activation of ETA and ETB receptors, and these neurons are implicated in vascular pain signalling. EDN1-expressing DRG neurons have been characterized in rat; their prevalence and properties in human DRG remain to be systematically determined, as EDN1 is not yet represented in human DRG single-cell transcriptomic datasets." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1073/pnas.86.19.7634" 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion endothelin-1 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079001) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "cervical dorsal root ganglion endothelin-1 neuron"

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion peripherin neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079004) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion peripherin neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079004) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A small-diameter sensory neuron whose soma is located in the cervical dorsal root ganglion and that expresses peripherin (encoded by PRPH), a type III intermediate filament protein. This neuron is unmyelinated, conducts action potentials as a C-fiber, and encompasses nociceptive and thermoceptive functional subpopulations. In human DRG, peripherin-immunoreactive neurons are predominantly of small diameter (Chang et al. 2018, PMID:28424991), distinguishing them from large-diameter myelinated A-fiber neurons that instead express neurofilament heavy chain (Haberberger et al. 2019, PMID:31293388). In mice, peripherin similarly marks unmyelinated primary afferent neurons." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1007/s12264-017-0132-3" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:31293388" 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion peripherin neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079004) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "cervical dorsal root ganglion peripherin neuron"

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion substance P neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079007) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A peptidergic nociceptor whose soma is located in the cervical dorsal root ganglion and that expresses substance P, an 11-amino-acid neuropeptide cleaved from protachykinin-1 (encoded by TAC1). This small-diameter neuron frequently co-expresses CGRP and belongs to the TrkA-positive peptidergic population. Upon nociceptive activation, it releases substance P both centrally in the spinal cord dorsal horn and peripherally at sensory nerve terminals, mediating neurogenic inflammation through NK1 receptor signalling (Haberberger et al. 2019, PMID:31293388). Substance P-immunoreactive neurons have been consistently identified in human DRG by immunohistochemistry across multiple studies." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1016/S0304-3940(00)00772-2" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:31293388" 

- [cervical dorsal root ganglion substance P neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079007) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "cervical dorsal root ganglion substance P neuron"

- [cholinergic neuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020057) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "An enteric neuron whose soma resides in the myenteric plexus and which is capable of acetylcholine secretion, neurotransmission. This is a defined grouping class that autoclassifies stubby Dogiel type I neurons, intrinsic primary afferent neurons, ascending interneurons, and their morphological/chemical subterms."

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "doi:10.1002/ar.21516"

#### Added
- [ciliary ganglion VIP/PHI neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4033139) [date](http://purl.org/dc/terms/date) "2026-03-13T11:13:54Z"^^[dateTime](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime) 

- [ciliary ganglion VIP/PHI neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4033139) [has exact synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasExactSynonym) "ciliary ganglion VIP-PHI neuron" 

- [ciliary ganglion VIP/PHI neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4033139) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A parasympathetic postganglionic neuron whose soma is located in the ciliary ganglion and that co-expresses vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP) and peptide histidine isoleucine (PHI, both encoded by the VIP gene). This neuron provides parasympathetic innervation to the eye, contributing to regulation of pupillary constriction and ciliary muscle accommodation. It has been identified in human ciliary ganglia by immunohistochemistry (Kiyokawa et al. 2012, PMID:22095632). VIP/PHI co-expression distinguishes this subpopulation from other cholinergic parasympathetic neurons in the ciliary ganglion and is characteristic of a subset of cranial parasympathetic neurons in humans and other mammals." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1002/ar.21516" 

- [ciliary ganglion VIP/PHI neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4033139) SubClassOf [parasympathetic neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0011102)

### deep fascia `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0011236`

#### Added
- Class: [deep fascia](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0011236) 

- [deep fascia](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0011236) [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "FMA:27935" 

- [deep fascia](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0011236) [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "NCIT:C32435" 

- [deep fascia](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0011236) [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "Wikipedia:Deep_fascia" 

- [deep fascia](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0011236) [id](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#id) "UBERON:0011236" 

- [deep fascia](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0011236) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "Deep fascia (or 'investing fascia') is a layer of fascia which can surround individual muscles, and divide groups of muscles into compartments. This is the dense fibrous connective tissue that interpenetrates and surrounds the muscles, bones, nerves and blood vessels of the body. It provides connection and communication in the form of aponeuroses, ligaments, tendons, retinacula, joint capsules, and septa. The deep fasciae envelop all bone; cartilage, and blood vessels and become specialized in muscles and nerves. The high density of collagen fibers is what gives the deep fascia its strength and integrity. The amount of elastin fiber determines how much extensibility and resilience it will have." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "Wikipedia:Deep_fascia" 

- [deep fascia](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0011236) [has narrow synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasNarrowSynonym) "deep investing fascia" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "Wikipedia:Deep_fascia" 

- [deep fascia](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0011236) [has_related_synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasRelatedSynonym) "fascia of muscles" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "Wikipedia:Deep_fascia" 

- [deep fascia](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0011236) [has_related_synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasRelatedSynonym) "investing fascia" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "Wikipedia:Deep_fascia" 

- [deep fascia](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0011236) [has_related_synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasRelatedSynonym) "deep fasciae" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "Wikipedia:Deep_fascia" 

  - [has_synonym_type](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasSynonymType) [plural form](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0003004) 

- [deep fascia](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0011236) [has exact synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasExactSynonym) "fascia profunda" 
  - [has_synonym_type](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasSynonymType) [OMO_0003011](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0003011) 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "Wikipedia:Deep_fascia" 

- [deep fascia](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0011236) [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "UMLS:C1511746" 
  - [source](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#source) "ncithesaurus:Deep_Fascia" 

- [deep fascia](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0011236) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "deep fascia" 

- [deep fascia](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0011236) SubClassOf [fascia](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0008982) 


### deep zone articular chondrocyte `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020039`

#### Added
- Class: [deep zone articular chondrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020039) 

- [deep zone articular chondrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020039) [contributor](http://purl.org/dc/terms/contributor) [0000-0003-0098-4399](https://orcid.org/0000-0003-0098-4399) 

- [deep zone articular chondrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020039) [date](http://purl.org/dc/terms/date) "2026-02-18T11:06:13Z"^^[dateTime](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime) 

- [deep zone articular chondrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020039) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [human_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#human_subset) 

- [deep zone articular chondrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020039) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [deep zone articular chondrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020039) [has_related_synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasRelatedSynonym) "radial zone articular chondrocyte" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:23015907" 

- [deep zone articular chondrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020039) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "An articular chondrocyte located in the deep (radial) zone of articular cartilage, characterized by columnar arrangement perpendicular to the joint surface and residence within a matrix containing the highest proteoglycan concentration and large-diameter collagen fibrils oriented radially. In humans, this cell expresses collagen type X (COL10A1) and provides maximal resistance to compressive forces. It is positioned adjacent to the tidemark, where collagen fibrils extend into the calcified cartilage to anchor the articular cartilage to subchondral bone." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:23015907" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:23124445" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:41226342" 

- [deep zone articular chondrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020039) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "deep zone articular chondrocyte" 

- [deep zone articular chondrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020039) SubClassOf [articular chondrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_1001607) 

- [deep zone articular chondrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020039) SubClassOf [expresses](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002292) some [collagen alpha-1(X) chain](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PR_000005693)

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:34170401" 

- [descending interneuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020051) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "An interneuron of the myenteric plexus whose axon projects aborally (in the descending direction) along the gut axis. This neuron class encompasses multiple chemically diverse subtypes including serotonergic (5-HT+), nitrergic (NOS1+), and other populations, forming the inhibitory limb of descending reflex pathways." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:40954253" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:34170401"

- [descending interneuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020051) SubClassOf [interneuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020047)

- [dorsal root ganglion BRN3A neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4033175) [date](http://purl.org/dc/terms/date) "2026-03-05T00:00:00Z"^^[dateTime](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime) 

- [dorsal root ganglion BRN3A neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4033175) [has exact synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasExactSynonym) "DRG BRN3A neuron" 

- [dorsal root ganglion BRN3A neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4033175) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [human_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#human_subset)

- [dorsal root ganglion BRN3A neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4033175) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A sensory neuron that has the soma located in the dorsal root ganglion and expresses the pan-sensory transcription factor POU domain, class 4, transcription factor 1 (BRN3A/POU4F1). In humans, BRN3A is expressed by approximately 98% of DRG neurons and serves as a broad marker for the sensory neuron population." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:29229553" 

- [dorsal root ganglion BRN3A neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4033175) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "dorsal root ganglion BRN3A neuron" 

- [dorsal root ganglion BRN3A neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4033175) SubClassOf [BFO_0000002](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000002) 

- [dorsal root ganglion BRN3A neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4033175) SubClassOf [sensory neuron of dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_1001451) 

- [dorsal root ganglion BRN3A neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4033175) SubClassOf [expresses](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002292) some [POU domain, class 4, transcription factor 1](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PR_000013041)

### fasciacyte `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020043`

#### Added
- Class: [fasciacyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020043) 

- [fasciacyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020043) [term tracker item](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000233) [3497](https://github.com/obophenotype/cell-ontology/issues/3497) 

- [fasciacyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020043) [contributor](http://purl.org/dc/terms/contributor) [0000-0002-5507-2103](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-5507-2103) 

- [fasciacyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020043) [date](http://purl.org/dc/terms/date) "2026-02-20T14:27:58Z"^^[dateTime](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime) 

- [fasciacyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020043) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [human_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#human_subset) 

- [fasciacyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020043) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [fasciacyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020043) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A mesenchymal stromal cell of the deep fascia (e.g., fascia lata) that is vimentin-positive, CD68-negative, and S-100A4-positive, organized in small clusters at the interface between fibrous fascial sublayers and loose connective tissue. It is specialized for hyaluronan-rich ECM biosynthesis, as evidenced by HAS2 mRNA expression, Alcian Blue staining, and anti-HABP immunoreactivity, and functions to regulate fascial gliding and viscoelasticity." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:33573365" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:29575206" 

- [fasciacyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020043) [comment](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#comment) "Stecco et al. demonstrate that fasciacytes differ from classical fascial fibroblasts in morphology, location (small clusters at the interface between fibrous and loose fascial layers), marker profile (S-100A4 expression), and ECM output (HA-rich rather than collagen-rich)." 

- [fasciacyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020043) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "fasciacyte" 

- [fasciacyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020043) EquivalentTo [stromal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0000499) and ([part of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000050) some [deep fascia](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0011236)) 

- [fasciacyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020043) SubClassOf [stromal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0000499) 

- [fasciacyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020043) SubClassOf [part of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000050) some [deep fascia](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0011236)

### has constituent monomer `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0020337`

#### Added
- [has constituent monomer](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0020337) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A relation that holds between a polymer and its constituent monomers. P has_constituent_monomer 'm' if and only if P has_component multiple instances of 'm' and each instance of 'm' is covalently attached to another instance of 'm'."@en 

- [has constituent monomer](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0020337) [comment](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#comment) "Usage note: The constituent monomers need not be covalently attached in an end-to-end manner; that is, polymers with internal connections (e.g., polyubiquitin) can use this relation, as can circular polymers (e.g., plasmids) and multi-branched polymers (e.g., glycans). This relation, as non-transitive, allows the number of instances of 'm' to be indicated." 

- [has constituent monomer](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0020337) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "has constituent monomer" 

- [has constituent monomer](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0020337) SubPropertyOf: [has component](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002180)

- [hepatocyte proliferation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0072574) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The multiplication or reproduction of hepatocytes, resulting in the expansion of a cell population. Hepatocytes form the main structural component of the liver. They are specialized epithelial cells that are organized into interconnected plates called lobules." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:mah" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "CL:0000182"

- [hepatocyte proliferation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0072574) EquivalentTo [cell population proliferation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0008283) and ([acts on population of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0012003) some [hepatocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0000182)) 

- [hepatocyte proliferation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0072574) SubClassOf [epithelial cell proliferation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0050673)

### hybrid osteochondral skeletal cell `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020028`

#### Added
- Class: [hybrid osteochondral skeletal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020028) 

- [hybrid osteochondral skeletal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020028) [present in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002175) [Mus musculus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCBITaxon_10090) 

- [hybrid osteochondral skeletal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020028) [contributor](http://purl.org/dc/terms/contributor) [0009-0000-8480-9277](https://orcid.org/0009-0000-8480-9277) 

- [hybrid osteochondral skeletal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020028) [creator](http://purl.org/dc/terms/creator) "GitHub Copilot" 

- [hybrid osteochondral skeletal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020028) [date](http://purl.org/dc/terms/date) "2025-12-16T14:37:04Z"^^[dateTime](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime) 

- [hybrid osteochondral skeletal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020028) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [hybrid osteochondral skeletal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020028) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A skeletal cell at the periosteal surface of the murine rib that displays hybrid osteochondral properties, emerging within the large callus that bridges segmental rib defects. It derives from a Sox9-expressing periosteal skeletal stem/progenitor subpopulation that constitutes only a small fraction of uninjured rib periosteum. After injury, these cells populate the callus and co-express cartilage and bone regulators Sox9 and Runx2, as well as matrix genes Col2a1 and Col1a1, while exhibiting dual chondrocyte/osteoblast properties." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:30983567" 

- [hybrid osteochondral skeletal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020028) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "hybrid osteochondral skeletal cell" 

- [hybrid osteochondral skeletal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020028) SubClassOf [bone cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0001035) 

- [hybrid osteochondral skeletal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020028) SubClassOf [skeletogenic cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0007001) 

- [hybrid osteochondral skeletal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020028) SubClassOf [part of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000050) some [periosteum](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002515) 

- [hybrid osteochondral skeletal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020028) SubClassOf [in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162) some [Mus musculus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCBITaxon_10090)

- [insulin receptor complex](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0005899) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A disulfide-bonded, heterotetrameric receptor complex. The alpha chains are entirely extracellular, while each beta chain has one transmembrane domain. The ligand binds to the alpha subunit extracellular domain and the kinase is associated with the beta subunit intracellular domain." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "ISBN:0198506732"

- [interneuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020047) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "An interneuron of the enteric nervous system whose soma resides in the myenteric plexus. Interneurons of the myenteric plexus integrate sensory input from intrinsic primary afferent neurons (IPANs) and modulate motor output to smooth muscle and secretory epithelia by synapsing onto motor neurons and other interneurons within the plexus." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:40954253" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:34170401"

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:34170401" 

- [interneuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020047) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "interneuron of myenteric plexus"

- [intestinal enterochromaffin enteroendocrine cell differentiation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_7770020) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The process in which relatively unspecialized cells acquire specialized structural and/or functional features of an intestinal enterochromaffin enteroendocrine cell. Enterochromaffin enteroendocrine cells are found in the gastrointestinal mucosa and secrete serotonin and some neurotransmitters including enkephalins and substance P." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:37883554"

- [intestinofugal neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020049) [creator](http://purl.org/dc/terms/creator) "GitHub Copilot" 

- [intestinofugal neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020049) [date](http://purl.org/dc/terms/date) "2026-03-10T14:42:58Z"^^[dateTime](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime) 

- [intestinofugal neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020049) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [human_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#human_subset) 

- [intestinofugal neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020049) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [intestinofugal neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020049) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "An enteric neuron whose soma resides in the myenteric plexus of the intestine and whose axon projects outside the gut wall to synapse on neurons in prevertebral sympathetic ganglia (celiac, superior mesenteric, or inferior mesenteric ganglia). This neuron provides a pathway for gut-to-brain communication via sympathetic prevertebral ganglia. In humans, 89% are immunopositive for choline acetyltransferase (ChAT); CART (cocaine- and amphetamine-regulated transcript) is NOT a human marker (0% CART+) but is present in rodent viscerofugal neurons." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:40954253" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:34170401" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:38292899" 

- [intestinofugal neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020049) [has exact synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasExactSynonym) "cholinergic viscerofugal neuron" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:38292899" 

- [intestinofugal neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020049) [has exact synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasExactSynonym) "viscerofugal neuron" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:38292899" 

- [intestinofugal neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020049) [has_related_synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasRelatedSynonym) "VFN" 
  - [has_synonym_type](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasSynonymType) [abbreviation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0003000) 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:38292899" 

- [intestinofugal neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020049) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "intestinofugal neuron" 

- [intestinofugal neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020049) SubClassOf [enteric neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0007011)

### intraepithelial lymphocyte `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0002496`
#### Removed
- [intraepithelial lymphocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0002496) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A T cell that is located in the intestinal epithelium and is capable of a mucosal immune response." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:tfm" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "MP:0008894" 

- [intraepithelial lymphocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0002496) EquivalentTo [mature T cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0002419) and ([located in](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0001025) some [intestinal epithelium](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001277)) and ([capable of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002215) some [mucosal immune response](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0002385)) 

- [intraepithelial lymphocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0002496) SubClassOf [located in](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0001025) some [intestinal epithelium](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001277) 

#### Added
- [intraepithelial lymphocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0002496) [contributor](http://purl.org/dc/terms/contributor) [0009-0000-8480-9277](https://orcid.org/0009-0000-8480-9277) 

- [intraepithelial lymphocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0002496) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A tissue-resident lymphocyte located within the epithelial layer of mucosal tissues, particularly within the gastrointestinal, respiratory, and reproductive tracts. Characterised by permanent residency, this cell typically expresses CD103 (integrin alpha-E), which binds to E-cadherin on epithelial cells, enabling epithelial retention in both mice and humans. Phenotypically, IEL displays an activated, antigen-experienced state characterised by the constitutive expression of cytotoxic molecules (granzyme B, perforin) and innate-like receptors (such as NKG2D), enabling rapid, localised surveillance and immediate response to epithelial stress." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:tfm" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:29674648" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "WIKIPEDIA:Intraepithelial_lymphocyte" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "MP:0008894" 

- [intraepithelial lymphocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0002496) EquivalentTo [mature T cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0002419) and ([located in](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0001025) some [epithelium](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000483)) and ([capable of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002215) some [mucosal immune response](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0002385)) 

- [intraepithelial lymphocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0002496) SubClassOf [located in](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0001025) some [epithelium](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000483) 


### intrinsic cardiac ganglion TH neuron `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079010`

#### Added
- Class: [intrinsic cardiac ganglion TH neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079010) 

- [intrinsic cardiac ganglion TH neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079010) [contributor](http://purl.org/dc/terms/contributor) [0000-0001-6677-8489](https://orcid.org/0000-0001-6677-8489) 

- [intrinsic cardiac ganglion TH neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079010) [date](http://purl.org/dc/terms/date) "2026-03-13T11:13:54Z"^^[dateTime](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime) 

- [intrinsic cardiac ganglion TH neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079010) [has_related_synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasRelatedSynonym) "catecholaminergic intrinsic cardiac neuron" 

- [intrinsic cardiac ganglion TH neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079010) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [human_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#human_subset) 

- [intrinsic cardiac ganglion TH neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079010) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [intrinsic cardiac ganglion TH neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079010) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A catecholaminergic neuron whose soma is located in an intrinsic cardiac ganglion and that expresses tyrosine hydroxylase (TH, encoded by TH), the rate-limiting enzyme in catecholamine biosynthesis. This neuron constitutes a minor subpopulation within the predominantly cholinergic parasympathetic intrinsic cardiac nervous system, identified in adult human cardiac ganglia by immunohistochemistry (Singh et al. 1999, PMID:9918529; Kawano et al. 2003, DOI:10.1007/s003800300005). It participates in local cardiac autonomic regulation, including modulation of heart rate and contractility. Its catecholaminergic phenotype distinguishes it from the majority cholinergic neurons of the same ganglia." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1007/s003800300005" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:9918529" 

- [intrinsic cardiac ganglion TH neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079010) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "intrinsic cardiac ganglion TH neuron" 

- [intrinsic cardiac ganglion TH neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079010) SubClassOf [autonomic neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0000107) 

- [intrinsic cardiac ganglion TH neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079010) SubClassOf [has soma location](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002100) some [cardiac ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0014463) 

- [intrinsic cardiac ganglion TH neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079010) SubClassOf [expresses](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002292) some [tyrosine 3-monooxygenase](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PR_000016301)

- [intrinsic primary afferent neuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020046) [has exact synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasExactSynonym) "myenteric sensory neuron" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:34170401" 

- [intrinsic primary afferent neuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020046) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A sensory neuron of the enteric nervous system whose soma resides in the myenteric plexus and which functions as the afferent limb of intrinsic reflex circuits controlling motility, secretion, and blood flow. This neuron is characterised by Dogiel type II morphology (large smooth soma with multiple long axon-like processes), AH-type electrophysiology (prolonged afterhyperpolarization following an action potential), and is immunopositive for choline acetyltransferase (ChAT) and immunonegative for neuronal nitric oxide synthase (NOS1)." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:40954253" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:34170401" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:37355216" 

- [intrinsic primary afferent neuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020046) [has_related_synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasRelatedSynonym) "IPAN" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:34170401" 

  - [has_synonym_type](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasSynonymType) [abbreviation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0003000) 

- [intrinsic primary afferent neuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020046) [has exact synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasExactSynonym) "multiaxonal cholinergic myenteric sensory neuron" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:37355216"

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion CGRP neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079011) [contributor](http://purl.org/dc/terms/contributor) [0000-0001-6677-8489](https://orcid.org/0000-0001-6677-8489) 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion CGRP neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079011) [date](http://purl.org/dc/terms/date) "2026-03-13T11:13:54Z"^^[dateTime](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime) 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion CGRP neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079011) [has_related_synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasRelatedSynonym) "peptidergic nociceptor" 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion CGRP neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079011) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [human_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#human_subset)
