- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion CGRP neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079011) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A peptidergic nociceptor whose soma is located in the lumbar dorsal root ganglion, characterized by expression of calcitonin gene-related peptide (CGRP, encoded by CALCA). This small- to medium-diameter neuron belongs to the TrkA-positive peptidergic subpopulation and typically co-expresses substance P. It innervates peripheral tissues including skin and viscera, where it mediates neurogenic inflammation and nociceptive signalling through release of CGRP from peripheral terminals during tissue injury (Haberberger et al. 2019, PMID:31293388). In human DRG, CGRP-immunoreactive neurons represent a substantial proportion of nociceptors, comparable to the pattern observed in rodents (Rostock et al. 2018, PMID:29229553)." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1016/S0304-3940(00)00772-2" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:31293388" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:29229553" 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion CGRP neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079011) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "lumbar dorsal root ganglion CGRP neuron" 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion CGRP neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079011) SubClassOf [BFO_0000002](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000002) 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion CGRP neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079011) SubClassOf [sensory neuron of dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_1001451) 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion CGRP neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079011) SubClassOf [has soma location](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002100) some [lumbar dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002836) 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion CGRP neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079011) SubClassOf [expresses](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002292) some [calcitonin gene-related peptide 1](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PR_000027226) 


### lumbar dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079013`

#### Added
- Class: [lumbar dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079013) 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079013) [contributor](http://purl.org/dc/terms/contributor) [0000-0001-6677-8489](https://orcid.org/0000-0001-6677-8489) 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079013) [date](http://purl.org/dc/terms/date) "2026-03-13T11:13:54Z"^^[dateTime](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime) 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079013) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [human_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#human_subset) 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079013) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079013) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A nociceptor whose soma is located in the lumbar dorsal root ganglion and that expresses the voltage-gated sodium channel Nav1.7 (encoded by SCN9A). This neuron is characterized by Nav1.7-dependent action potential initiation and amplification, which is essential for normal pain sensation in humans. Nav1.7 contributes approximately 50% of total sodium channel current in human DRG nociceptors (Chang et al. 2018, PMID:28424991). In human DRG, Nav1.7 immunoreactivity is detected in 54-57% of TrkA-positive neurons (Rostock et al. 2018, PMID:29229553). Human genetic evidence confirms Nav1.7 as necessary and sufficient for nociceptive function in these neurons." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1007/s12264-017-0132-3" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:29229553" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1038/nn.3602" 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079013) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "lumbar dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron" 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079013) SubClassOf [BFO_0000002](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000002) 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079013) SubClassOf [sensory neuron of dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_1001451) 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079013) SubClassOf [has soma location](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002100) some [lumbar dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002836) 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079013) SubClassOf [expresses](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002292) some [sodium channel protein type 9 subunit alpha](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PR_000002104)

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion Nav1.9 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079014) [date](http://purl.org/dc/terms/date) "2026-03-13T11:13:54Z"^^[dateTime](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime)

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion Nav1.9 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079014) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A nociceptor whose soma is located in the lumbar dorsal root ganglion and that expresses the voltage-gated sodium channel Nav1.9 (encoded by SCN11A). This neuron is characterized by a persistent, tetrodotoxin-resistant sodium current that modulates resting membrane potential and amplifies subthreshold depolarizations, contributing to enhanced excitability during inflammation (Dib-Hajj et al. 2002, PMID:11972962). In human DRG, Nav1.9-immunoreactive neurons constitute approximately 26% of TrkA-positive neurons, a significantly higher proportion than the 12% observed in mouse (Rostock et al. 2018, PMID:29229553), suggesting species differences in inflammatory pain processing." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:29229553" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1038/nn.3602" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1016/S0166-2236(02)02150-1" 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion Nav1.9 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079014) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "lumbar dorsal root ganglion Nav1.9 neuron" 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion Nav1.9 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079014) SubClassOf [BFO_0000002](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000002) 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion Nav1.9 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079014) SubClassOf [sensory neuron of dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_1001451)

### lumbar dorsal root ganglion Piezo2 neuron `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079016`

#### Added
- Class: [lumbar dorsal root ganglion Piezo2 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079016) 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion Piezo2 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079016) [contributor](http://purl.org/dc/terms/contributor) [0000-0001-6677-8489](https://orcid.org/0000-0001-6677-8489) 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion Piezo2 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079016) [date](http://purl.org/dc/terms/date) "2026-03-13T11:13:54Z"^^[dateTime](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime) 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion Piezo2 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079016) [has_related_synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasRelatedSynonym) "mechanosensitive DRG neuron" 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion Piezo2 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079016) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [human_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#human_subset) 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion Piezo2 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079016) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion Piezo2 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079016) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A mechanosensitive sensory neuron whose soma is located in the lumbar dorsal root ganglion and that expresses the mechanically activated ion channel Piezo2 (encoded by PIEZO2). This neuron transduces mechanical stimuli and is involved in light touch perception and proprioception. In human DRG, Piezo2-immunoreactive neurons constitute approximately 35% of TrkA-positive neurons, compared to 26% in mouse (Rostock et al. 2018, PMID:29229553). This subpopulation includes both low-threshold mechanoreceptors mediating innocuous touch and a subset of mechanically sensitive nociceptors, reflecting the dual role of Piezo2 in somatosensation across species." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:29229553" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1016/j.neuroscience.2017.11.047" 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion Piezo2 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079016) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "lumbar dorsal root ganglion Piezo2 neuron"

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion RET neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079017) [date](http://purl.org/dc/terms/date) "2026-03-13T11:13:54Z"^^[dateTime](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime) 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion RET neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079017) [has_related_synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasRelatedSynonym) "non-peptidergic nociceptor" 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion RET neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079017) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [human_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#human_subset) 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion RET neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079017) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion RET neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079017) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A non-peptidergic nociceptor whose soma is located in the lumbar dorsal root ganglion and that expresses the receptor tyrosine kinase RET (encoded by RET). This neuron is distinguished from peptidergic nociceptors by its dependence on glial cell-derived neurotrophic factor family ligands rather than nerve growth factor, and in rodents it characteristically binds isolectin B4. In human DRG, RET-immunoreactive neurons constitute approximately 46% of TrkA-positive neurons, a significantly higher proportion than the 23% observed in mouse (Rostock et al. 2018, PMID:29229553), indicating an expanded non-peptidergic nociceptor compartment in humans." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:29229553" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1016/j.neuroscience.2017.11.047" 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion RET neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079017) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "lumbar dorsal root ganglion RET neuron" 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion RET neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079017) SubClassOf [BFO_0000002](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000002) 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion RET neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079017) SubClassOf [sensory neuron of dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_1001451) 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion RET neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079017) SubClassOf [has soma location](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002100) some [lumbar dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002836) 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion RET neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079017) SubClassOf [expresses](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002292) some [proto-oncogene tyrosine-protein kinase receptor Ret](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PR_000013895)

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion TRPV1 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079019) [date](http://purl.org/dc/terms/date) "2026-03-13T11:13:54Z"^^[dateTime](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime) 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion TRPV1 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079019) [has_related_synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasRelatedSynonym) "capsaicin-sensitive nociceptor" 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion TRPV1 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079019) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [human_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#human_subset) 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion TRPV1 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079019) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion TRPV1 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079019) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A polymodal nociceptor whose soma is located in the lumbar dorsal root ganglion and that expresses the transient receptor potential vanilloid 1 channel (TRPV1, encoded by TRPV1). This neuron detects noxious heat above 43 degrees Celsius, protons, and endogenous lipid mediators, and is activated by capsaicin. In human DRG, TRPV1-immunoreactive neurons constitute approximately 54% of TrkA-positive neurons, significantly higher than the 35% observed in mouse (Rostock et al. 2018, PMID:29229553). These neurons are predominantly small-diameter nociceptors that innervate skin and visceral organs, where they function as primary detectors of thermal and chemical noxious stimuli." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1007/s12264-017-0132-3" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:29229553" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1016/j.neuroscience.2017.11.047" 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion TRPV1 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079019) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "lumbar dorsal root ganglion TRPV1 neuron" 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion TRPV1 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079019) SubClassOf [BFO_0000002](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000002) 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion TRPV1 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079019) SubClassOf [sensory neuron of dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_1001451)

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion endothelin-1 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079012) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A sensory neuron whose soma is located in the lumbar dorsal root ganglion and that expresses endothelin-1 (EDN1). In rat DRG, EDN1 immunoreactivity has been detected in a subset of neuron somata (Giaid et al. 1989, PMID:2678100). Endothelin-1 acts as an endogenous pain mediator, sensitizing nociceptors through activation of ETA and ETB receptors, and these neurons are implicated in vascular pain signalling. EDN1-expressing DRG neurons have been characterized in rat; their prevalence and properties in human DRG remain to be systematically determined, as EDN1 is not yet represented in human DRG single-cell transcriptomic datasets." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1073/pnas.86.19.7634" 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion endothelin-1 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079012) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "lumbar dorsal root ganglion endothelin-1 neuron" 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion endothelin-1 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079012) SubClassOf [BFO_0000002](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000002) 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion endothelin-1 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079012) SubClassOf [sensory neuron of dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_1001451) 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion endothelin-1 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079012) SubClassOf [has soma location](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002100) some [lumbar dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002836)

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion peripherin neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079015) [has exact synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasExactSynonym) "lumbar dorsal root ganglion Peripherin neuron" 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion peripherin neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079015) [has_related_synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasRelatedSynonym) "C-fiber neuron" 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion peripherin neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079015) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [human_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#human_subset) 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion peripherin neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079015) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion peripherin neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079015) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A small-diameter sensory neuron whose soma is located in the lumbar dorsal root ganglion and that expresses peripherin (encoded by PRPH), a type III intermediate filament protein. This neuron is unmyelinated, conducts action potentials as a C-fiber, and encompasses nociceptive and thermoceptive functional subpopulations. In human DRG, peripherin-immunoreactive neurons are predominantly of small diameter (Chang et al. 2018, PMID:28424991), distinguishing them from large-diameter myelinated A-fiber neurons that instead express neurofilament heavy chain (Haberberger et al. 2019, PMID:31293388). In mice, peripherin similarly marks unmyelinated primary afferent neurons." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1007/s12264-017-0132-3" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:31293388" 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion peripherin neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079015) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "lumbar dorsal root ganglion peripherin neuron" 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion peripherin neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079015) SubClassOf [BFO_0000002](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000002) 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion peripherin neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079015) SubClassOf [sensory neuron of dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_1001451) 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion peripherin neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079015) SubClassOf [has soma location](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002100) some [lumbar dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002836)

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion substance P neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079018) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion substance P neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079018) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A peptidergic nociceptor whose soma is located in the lumbar dorsal root ganglion and that expresses substance P, an 11-amino-acid neuropeptide cleaved from protachykinin-1 (encoded by TAC1). This small-diameter neuron frequently co-expresses CGRP and belongs to the TrkA-positive peptidergic population. Upon nociceptive activation, it releases substance P both centrally in the spinal cord dorsal horn and peripherally at sensory nerve terminals, mediating neurogenic inflammation through NK1 receptor signalling (Haberberger et al. 2019, PMID:31293388). Substance P-immunoreactive neurons have been consistently identified in human DRG by immunohistochemistry across multiple studies." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1016/S0304-3940(00)00772-2" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:31293388" 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion substance P neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079018) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "lumbar dorsal root ganglion substance P neuron" 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion substance P neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079018) SubClassOf [BFO_0000002](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000002) 

- [lumbar dorsal root ganglion substance P neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079018) SubClassOf [sensory neuron of dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_1001451)

- [middle zone articular chondrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020038) [has_related_synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasRelatedSynonym) "transitional zone articular chondrocyte" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:23015907" 

- [middle zone articular chondrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020038) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "An articular chondrocyte located in the middle (transitional) zone of articular cartilage, characterized by round morphology and relatively random spatial organization, and residing in a matrix enriched in proteoglycans and thicker collagen fibrils oriented obliquely to the articular surface. This cell contributes to the largest volume fraction of articular cartilage and functions primarily in distributing and absorbing compressive loads between the superficial and deep zones." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:23015907" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:23124445" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:41226342" 

- [middle zone articular chondrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020038) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "middle zone articular chondrocyte" 

- [middle zone articular chondrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020038) SubClassOf [articular chondrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_1001607)

### retinal ganglion cell A `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0004117`
#### Removed
- [retinal ganglion cell A](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0004117) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A monostratified retinal ganglion cell with large soma and large dendritic field." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:12209831" 

- [retinal ganglion cell A](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0004117) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "retinal ganglion cell A" 

#### Added
- [retinal ganglion cell A](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0004117) [has exact synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasExactSynonym) "retinal ganglion cell A" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:12209831" 

- [retinal ganglion cell A](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0004117) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A large-bodied retinal projection neuron with wide monostratified dendritic arbors in defined IPL strata, high neurofilament and osteopontin expression, and a thick, fast-conducting axon. It shows short-latency, non-direction-selective responses with large receptive fields and a distinctive rapid action potential waveform. In mammals it forms about five percent of RGCs and includes four conserved ON and OFF sustained and transient subtypes." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:28753612" 

- [retinal ganglion cell A](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0004117) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "alpha retinal ganglion cell (Mmus)"

- [sacral dorsal root ganglion CGRP neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079020) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A peptidergic nociceptor whose soma is located in the sacral dorsal root ganglion, characterized by expression of calcitonin gene-related peptide (CGRP, encoded by CALCA). This small- to medium-diameter neuron belongs to the TrkA-positive peptidergic subpopulation and typically co-expresses substance P. It innervates peripheral tissues including skin and viscera, where it mediates neurogenic inflammation and nociceptive signalling through release of CGRP from peripheral terminals during tissue injury (Haberberger et al. 2019, PMID:31293388). In human DRG, CGRP-immunoreactive neurons represent a substantial proportion of nociceptors, comparable to the pattern observed in rodents (Rostock et al. 2018, PMID:29229553)." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1016/S0304-3940(00)00772-2" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:31293388" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:29229553" 

- [sacral dorsal root ganglion CGRP neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079020) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "sacral dorsal root ganglion CGRP neuron" 

- [sacral dorsal root ganglion CGRP neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079020) SubClassOf [BFO_0000002](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000002) 

- [sacral dorsal root ganglion CGRP neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079020) SubClassOf [sensory neuron of dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_1001451)

- [sacral dorsal root ganglion CGRP neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079020) SubClassOf [expresses](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002292) some [calcitonin gene-related peptide 1](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PR_000027226)

- [sacral dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079022) [date](http://purl.org/dc/terms/date) "2026-03-13T11:13:54Z"^^[dateTime](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime) 

- [sacral dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079022) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [human_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#human_subset) 

- [sacral dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079022) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [sacral dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079022) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A nociceptor whose soma is located in the sacral dorsal root ganglion and that expresses the voltage-gated sodium channel Nav1.7 (encoded by SCN9A). This neuron is characterized by Nav1.7-dependent action potential initiation and amplification, which is essential for normal pain sensation in humans. Nav1.7 contributes approximately 50% of total sodium channel current in human DRG nociceptors (Chang et al. 2018, PMID:28424991). In human DRG, Nav1.7 immunoreactivity is detected in 54-57% of TrkA-positive neurons (Rostock et al. 2018, PMID:29229553). Human genetic evidence confirms Nav1.7 as necessary and sufficient for nociceptive function in these neurons." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1007/s12264-017-0132-3" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:29229553" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1038/nn.3602" 

- [sacral dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079022) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "sacral dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron"

- [sacral dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079022) SubClassOf [sensory neuron of dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_1001451)

- [sacral dorsal root ganglion Nav1.9 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079023) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [sacral dorsal root ganglion Nav1.9 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079023) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A nociceptor whose soma is located in the sacral dorsal root ganglion and that expresses the voltage-gated sodium channel Nav1.9 (encoded by SCN11A). This neuron is characterized by a persistent, tetrodotoxin-resistant sodium current that modulates resting membrane potential and amplifies subthreshold depolarizations, contributing to enhanced excitability during inflammation (Dib-Hajj et al. 2002, PMID:11972962). In human DRG, Nav1.9-immunoreactive neurons constitute approximately 26% of TrkA-positive neurons, a significantly higher proportion than the 12% observed in mouse (Rostock et al. 2018, PMID:29229553), suggesting species differences in inflammatory pain processing." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:29229553" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1038/nn.3602" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1016/S0166-2236(02)02150-1"

- [sacral dorsal root ganglion Piezo2 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079025) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A mechanosensitive sensory neuron whose soma is located in the sacral dorsal root ganglion and that expresses the mechanically activated ion channel Piezo2 (encoded by PIEZO2). This neuron transduces mechanical stimuli and is involved in light touch perception and proprioception. In human DRG, Piezo2-immunoreactive neurons constitute approximately 35% of TrkA-positive neurons, compared to 26% in mouse (Rostock et al. 2018, PMID:29229553). This subpopulation includes both low-threshold mechanoreceptors mediating innocuous touch and a subset of mechanically sensitive nociceptors, reflecting the dual role of Piezo2 in somatosensation across species." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:29229553" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1016/j.neuroscience.2017.11.047" 

- [sacral dorsal root ganglion Piezo2 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079025) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "sacral dorsal root ganglion Piezo2 neuron"

- [sacral dorsal root ganglion RET neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079026) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A non-peptidergic nociceptor whose soma is located in the sacral dorsal root ganglion and that expresses the receptor tyrosine kinase RET (encoded by RET). This neuron is distinguished from peptidergic nociceptors by its dependence on glial cell-derived neurotrophic factor family ligands rather than nerve growth factor, and in rodents it characteristically binds isolectin B4. In human DRG, RET-immunoreactive neurons constitute approximately 46% of TrkA-positive neurons, a significantly higher proportion than the 23% observed in mouse (Rostock et al. 2018, PMID:29229553), indicating an expanded non-peptidergic nociceptor compartment in humans." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:29229553" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1016/j.neuroscience.2017.11.047" 

- [sacral dorsal root ganglion RET neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079026) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "sacral dorsal root ganglion RET neuron"

- [sacral dorsal root ganglion TRPV1 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079028) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [sacral dorsal root ganglion TRPV1 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079028) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A polymodal nociceptor whose soma is located in the sacral dorsal root ganglion and that expresses the transient receptor potential vanilloid 1 channel (TRPV1, encoded by TRPV1). This neuron detects noxious heat above 43 degrees Celsius, protons, and endogenous lipid mediators, and is activated by capsaicin. In human DRG, TRPV1-immunoreactive neurons constitute approximately 54% of TrkA-positive neurons, significantly higher than the 35% observed in mouse (Rostock et al. 2018, PMID:29229553). These neurons are predominantly small-diameter nociceptors that innervate skin and visceral organs, where they function as primary detectors of thermal and chemical noxious stimuli." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1007/s12264-017-0132-3" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:29229553" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1016/j.neuroscience.2017.11.047" 

- [sacral dorsal root ganglion TRPV1 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079028) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "sacral dorsal root ganglion TRPV1 neuron"

- [sacral dorsal root ganglion endothelin-1 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079021) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A sensory neuron whose soma is located in the sacral dorsal root ganglion and that expresses endothelin-1 (EDN1). In rat DRG, EDN1 immunoreactivity has been detected in a subset of neuron somata (Giaid et al. 1989, PMID:2678100). Endothelin-1 acts as an endogenous pain mediator, sensitizing nociceptors through activation of ETA and ETB receptors, and these neurons are implicated in vascular pain signalling. EDN1-expressing DRG neurons have been characterized in rat; their prevalence and properties in human DRG remain to be systematically determined, as EDN1 is not yet represented in human DRG single-cell transcriptomic datasets." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1073/pnas.86.19.7634" 

- [sacral dorsal root ganglion endothelin-1 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079021) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "sacral dorsal root ganglion endothelin-1 neuron"

- [sacral dorsal root ganglion peripherin neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079024) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [sacral dorsal root ganglion peripherin neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079024) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A small-diameter sensory neuron whose soma is located in the sacral dorsal root ganglion and that expresses peripherin (encoded by PRPH), a type III intermediate filament protein. This neuron is unmyelinated, conducts action potentials as a C-fiber, and encompasses nociceptive and thermoceptive functional subpopulations. In human DRG, peripherin-immunoreactive neurons are predominantly of small diameter (Chang et al. 2018, PMID:28424991), distinguishing them from large-diameter myelinated A-fiber neurons that instead express neurofilament heavy chain (Haberberger et al. 2019, PMID:31293388). In mice, peripherin similarly marks unmyelinated primary afferent neurons." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1007/s12264-017-0132-3" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:31293388" 

- [sacral dorsal root ganglion peripherin neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079024) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "sacral dorsal root ganglion peripherin neuron" 

- [sacral dorsal root ganglion peripherin neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079024) SubClassOf [BFO_0000002](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000002) 

- [sacral dorsal root ganglion peripherin neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079024) SubClassOf [sensory neuron of dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_1001451) 

- [sacral dorsal root ganglion peripherin neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079024) SubClassOf [has soma location](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002100) some [sacral dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002837) 

- [sacral dorsal root ganglion peripherin neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079024) SubClassOf [expresses](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002292) some [peripherin](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PR_000013289)

- [sacral dorsal root ganglion substance P neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079027) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [human_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#human_subset) 

- [sacral dorsal root ganglion substance P neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079027) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [sacral dorsal root ganglion substance P neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079027) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A peptidergic nociceptor whose soma is located in the sacral dorsal root ganglion and that expresses substance P, an 11-amino-acid neuropeptide cleaved from protachykinin-1 (encoded by TAC1). This small-diameter neuron frequently co-expresses CGRP and belongs to the TrkA-positive peptidergic population. Upon nociceptive activation, it releases substance P both centrally in the spinal cord dorsal horn and peripherally at sensory nerve terminals, mediating neurogenic inflammation through NK1 receptor signalling (Haberberger et al. 2019, PMID:31293388). Substance P-immunoreactive neurons have been consistently identified in human DRG by immunohistochemistry across multiple studies." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1016/S0304-3940(00)00772-2" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:31293388" 

- [sacral dorsal root ganglion substance P neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079027) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "sacral dorsal root ganglion substance P neuron" 

- [sacral dorsal root ganglion substance P neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079027) SubClassOf [BFO_0000002](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000002) 

- [sacral dorsal root ganglion substance P neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079027) SubClassOf [sensory neuron of dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_1001451) 

- [sacral dorsal root ganglion substance P neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079027) SubClassOf [has soma location](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002100) some [sacral dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002837) 

- [sacral dorsal root ganglion substance P neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079027) SubClassOf [expresses](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002292) some [protachykinin-1](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PR_000016003)

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:32888429" 

- [secretomotor/vasodilator neuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020048) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "An enteric neuron whose soma resides in the myenteric plexus and which controls mucosal secretion and blood flow by innervating secretory epithelia and submucosal blood vessels. This neuron is characterised by expression of vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP). In mouse, two Glp2r+ subtypes have been identified: PSVN1 (VIP+, non-cholinergic) and PSVN2 (ChAT+, cholinergic). In human, only the VIP+ non-cholinergic subtype has been detected." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:40954253" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:34170401" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:32888429"

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:34170401" 

  - [has_synonym_type](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasSynonymType) [abbreviation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0003000) 

- [secretomotor/vasodilator neuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020048) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "secretomotor/vasodilator neuron of myenteric plexus"

### skeletal muscle satellite stem cell maintenance involved in skeletal muscle regeneration `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0098731`
#### Removed
- Class: [skeletal muscle satellite stem cell maintenance involved in skeletal muscle regeneration](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0098731) 

- [skeletal muscle satellite stem cell maintenance involved in skeletal muscle regeneration](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0098731) [has_obo_namespace](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasOBONamespace) "biological_process" 

- [skeletal muscle satellite stem cell maintenance involved in skeletal muscle regeneration](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0098731) [id](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#id) "GO:0098731" 

- [skeletal muscle satellite stem cell maintenance involved in skeletal muscle regeneration](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0098731) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "Any process by which the number of skeletal muscle satellite stem cells in a skeletal muscle is maintained during skeletal muscle regeneration. There are at least two mechanisms by which this is achieved. Skeletal muscle satellite stem cell asymmetric division ensures satellite stem cell numbers are kept constant. Symmetric division of these cells amplifies the number of skeletal muscle satellite stem cells." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:23303905" 

- [skeletal muscle satellite stem cell maintenance involved in skeletal muscle regeneration](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0098731) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "skeletal muscle satellite stem cell maintenance involved in skeletal muscle regeneration"

- [skeletal muscle satellite stem cell maintenance involved in skeletal muscle regeneration](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0098731) SubClassOf [skeletal muscle satellite cell maintenance involved in skeletal muscle regeneration](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0014834) 

- [skeletal muscle satellite stem cell maintenance involved in skeletal muscle regeneration](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0098731) SubClassOf [somatic stem cell population maintenance](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0035019)

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:34170401" 

- [spiny Dogiel type I neuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020053) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A Dogiel type I neuron of the myenteric plexus characterised by spiny (spine-like) dendrite morphology with numerous short projections along the dendrites. This neuron is immunopositive for neuronal nitric oxide synthase (NOS1) and immunonegative for choline acetyltransferase (ChAT), corresponding to inhibitory motor neurons of the enteric nervous system." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:34170401" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:37355216" 

- [spiny Dogiel type I neuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020053) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "spiny Dogiel type I neuron of myenteric plexus" 

- [spiny Dogiel type I neuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020053) SubClassOf [dogiel type I neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4047038)

- [stem cell memory CD8-positive, alpha-beta T cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020034) [has exact synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasExactSynonym) "stem cell&ndash;like memory CD8-positive, alpha-beta T cell" 

- [stem cell memory CD8-positive, alpha-beta T cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020034) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [human_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#human_subset) 

- [stem cell memory CD8-positive, alpha-beta T cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020034) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [stem cell memory CD8-positive, alpha-beta T cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020034) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A CD8-positive memory alpha-beta T cell with stem-like properties that is long-lived, retains a na&iuml;ve-like phenotype, and exhibits self-renewal and multipotent differentiation capacity. This cell acts as a stem-like reservoir capable of regenerating central and effector memory T cell subsets." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:21926977" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:28060797" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:19525962" 

- [stem cell memory CD8-positive, alpha-beta T cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020034) [has exact synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasExactSynonym) "CD8+ T memory stem cell" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:21926977" 

- [stem cell memory CD8-positive, alpha-beta T cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020034) [has exact synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasExactSynonym) "CD8+ TSCM cell" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:21926977" 

- [stem cell memory CD8-positive, alpha-beta T cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020034) [has exact synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasExactSynonym) "CD8-positive TSCM cell" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:21926977" 

- [stem cell memory CD8-positive, alpha-beta T cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020034) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "stem cell memory CD8-positive, alpha-beta T cell" 

- [stem cell memory CD8-positive, alpha-beta T cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020034) SubClassOf [CD8-positive, alpha-beta memory T cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0000909)

- [stubby Dogiel type I neuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020052) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [stubby Dogiel type I neuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020052) [has exact synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasExactSynonym) "lamellar Dogiel type I neuron" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:34170401" 

- [stubby Dogiel type I neuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020052) [has exact synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasExactSynonym) "stubby Dogiel I neuron" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:34170401" 

- [stubby Dogiel type I neuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020052) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A Dogiel type I neuron of the myenteric plexus characterised by stubby (lamellar) dendrite morphology with broad, flattened dendritic expansions. This neuron is immunopositive for choline acetyltransferase (ChAT) and immunonegative for neuronal nitric oxide synthase (NOS1), corresponding to excitatory motor neurons of the enteric nervous system." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:34170401" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:37355216" 

- [stubby Dogiel type I neuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020052) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "stubby Dogiel type I neuron of myenteric plexus" 

- [stubby Dogiel type I neuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020052) SubClassOf [motor neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0000100) 

- [stubby Dogiel type I neuron of myenteric plexus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020052) SubClassOf [dogiel type I neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4047038)

- [superficial zone articular chondrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020037) [date](http://purl.org/dc/terms/date) "2026-02-18T11:06:13Z"^^[dateTime](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime) 

- [superficial zone articular chondrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020037) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [human_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#human_subset) 

- [superficial zone articular chondrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020037) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [superficial zone articular chondrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020037) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "An articular chondrocyte located in the most superficial zone of articular cartilage, characterized by flattened morphology and tangential alignment parallel to the joint surface, and residing within a matrix of fine collagen fibrils and relatively low proteoglycan content. In mice and humans, this cell typically expresses lubricin (PRG4) and contributes to lubrication and resistance to shear and tensile forces at the cartilage surface." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:23015907" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:23124445" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:41226342" 

- [superficial zone articular chondrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020037) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "superficial zone articular chondrocyte" 

- [superficial zone articular chondrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020037) SubClassOf [articular chondrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_1001607) 

- [superficial zone articular chondrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020037) SubClassOf [expresses](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002292) some [proteoglycan 4](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PR_000013208)

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion CGRP neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079029) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A peptidergic nociceptor whose soma is located in the thoracic dorsal root ganglion, characterized by expression of calcitonin gene-related peptide (CGRP, encoded by CALCA). This small- to medium-diameter neuron belongs to the TrkA-positive peptidergic subpopulation and typically co-expresses substance P. It innervates peripheral tissues including skin and viscera, where it mediates neurogenic inflammation and nociceptive signalling through release of CGRP from peripheral terminals during tissue injury (Haberberger et al. 2019, PMID:31293388). In human DRG, CGRP-immunoreactive neurons represent a substantial proportion of nociceptors, comparable to the pattern observed in rodents (Rostock et al. 2018, PMID:29229553)." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1016/S0304-3940(00)00772-2" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:31293388" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:29229553" 

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion CGRP neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079029) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "thoracic dorsal root ganglion CGRP neuron" 

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion CGRP neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079029) SubClassOf [BFO_0000002](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000002) 

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion CGRP neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079029) SubClassOf [sensory neuron of dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_1001451) 

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion CGRP neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079029) SubClassOf [has soma location](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002100) some [thoracic dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002835) 

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion CGRP neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079029) SubClassOf [expresses](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002292) some [calcitonin gene-related peptide 1](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PR_000027226) 


### thoracic dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079031`

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079031) [contributor](http://purl.org/dc/terms/contributor) [0000-0001-6677-8489](https://orcid.org/0000-0001-6677-8489) 

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079031) [date](http://purl.org/dc/terms/date) "2026-03-13T11:13:54Z"^^[dateTime](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime) 

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079031) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [human_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#human_subset) 

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079031) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079031) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A nociceptor whose soma is located in the thoracic dorsal root ganglion and that expresses the voltage-gated sodium channel Nav1.7 (encoded by SCN9A). This neuron is characterized by Nav1.7-dependent action potential initiation and amplification, which is essential for normal pain sensation in humans. Nav1.7 contributes approximately 50% of total sodium channel current in human DRG nociceptors (Chang et al. 2018, PMID:28424991). In human DRG, Nav1.7 immunoreactivity is detected in 54-57% of TrkA-positive neurons (Rostock et al. 2018, PMID:29229553). Human genetic evidence confirms Nav1.7 as necessary and sufficient for nociceptive function in these neurons." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1007/s12264-017-0132-3" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:29229553" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1038/nn.3602" 

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079031) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "thoracic dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron" 

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079031) SubClassOf [BFO_0000002](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000002) 

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079031) SubClassOf [sensory neuron of dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_1001451) 

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079031) SubClassOf [has soma location](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002100) some [thoracic dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002835) 

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion Nav1.7 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079031) SubClassOf [expresses](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002292) some [sodium channel protein type 9 subunit alpha](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PR_000002104)

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion Nav1.9 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079032) [date](http://purl.org/dc/terms/date) "2026-03-13T11:13:54Z"^^[dateTime](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime)

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion Nav1.9 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079032) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A nociceptor whose soma is located in the thoracic dorsal root ganglion and that expresses the voltage-gated sodium channel Nav1.9 (encoded by SCN11A). This neuron is characterized by a persistent, tetrodotoxin-resistant sodium current that modulates resting membrane potential and amplifies subthreshold depolarizations, contributing to enhanced excitability during inflammation (Dib-Hajj et al. 2002, PMID:11972962). In human DRG, Nav1.9-immunoreactive neurons constitute approximately 26% of TrkA-positive neurons, a significantly higher proportion than the 12% observed in mouse (Rostock et al. 2018, PMID:29229553), suggesting species differences in inflammatory pain processing." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:29229553" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1038/nn.3602" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1016/S0166-2236(02)02150-1"

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion Piezo2 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079034) [has_related_synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasRelatedSynonym) "mechanosensitive DRG neuron"

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion Piezo2 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079034) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion Piezo2 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079034) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A mechanosensitive sensory neuron whose soma is located in the thoracic dorsal root ganglion and that expresses the mechanically activated ion channel Piezo2 (encoded by PIEZO2). This neuron transduces mechanical stimuli and is involved in light touch perception and proprioception. In human DRG, Piezo2-immunoreactive neurons constitute approximately 35% of TrkA-positive neurons, compared to 26% in mouse (Rostock et al. 2018, PMID:29229553). This subpopulation includes both low-threshold mechanoreceptors mediating innocuous touch and a subset of mechanically sensitive nociceptors, reflecting the dual role of Piezo2 in somatosensation across species." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:29229553" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1016/j.neuroscience.2017.11.047" 

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion Piezo2 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079034) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "thoracic dorsal root ganglion Piezo2 neuron" 

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion Piezo2 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079034) SubClassOf [BFO_0000002](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000002) 

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion Piezo2 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079034) SubClassOf [sensory neuron of dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_1001451) 

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion Piezo2 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079034) SubClassOf [has soma location](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002100) some [thoracic dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002835)

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion RET neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079035) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A non-peptidergic nociceptor whose soma is located in the thoracic dorsal root ganglion and that expresses the receptor tyrosine kinase RET (encoded by RET). This neuron is distinguished from peptidergic nociceptors by its dependence on glial cell-derived neurotrophic factor family ligands rather than nerve growth factor, and in rodents it characteristically binds isolectin B4. In human DRG, RET-immunoreactive neurons constitute approximately 46% of TrkA-positive neurons, a significantly higher proportion than the 23% observed in mouse (Rostock et al. 2018, PMID:29229553), indicating an expanded non-peptidergic nociceptor compartment in humans." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:29229553" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1016/j.neuroscience.2017.11.047" 

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion RET neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079035) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "thoracic dorsal root ganglion RET neuron" 

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion RET neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079035) SubClassOf [BFO_0000002](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000002) 

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion RET neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079035) SubClassOf [sensory neuron of dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_1001451) 

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion RET neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079035) SubClassOf [has soma location](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002100) some [thoracic dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002835)

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion TRPV1 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079037) [has_related_synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasRelatedSynonym) "capsaicin-sensitive nociceptor"

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion TRPV1 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079037) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion TRPV1 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079037) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A polymodal nociceptor whose soma is located in the thoracic dorsal root ganglion and that expresses the transient receptor potential vanilloid 1 channel (TRPV1, encoded by TRPV1). This neuron detects noxious heat above 43 degrees Celsius, protons, and endogenous lipid mediators, and is activated by capsaicin. In human DRG, TRPV1-immunoreactive neurons constitute approximately 54% of TrkA-positive neurons, significantly higher than the 35% observed in mouse (Rostock et al. 2018, PMID:29229553). These neurons are predominantly small-diameter nociceptors that innervate skin and visceral organs, where they function as primary detectors of thermal and chemical noxious stimuli." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1007/s12264-017-0132-3" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:29229553" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1016/j.neuroscience.2017.11.047" 

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion TRPV1 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079037) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "thoracic dorsal root ganglion TRPV1 neuron"

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion endothelin-1 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079030) [has_related_synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasRelatedSynonym) "EDN1 neuron" 

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion endothelin-1 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079030) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [human_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#human_subset) 

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion endothelin-1 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079030) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion endothelin-1 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079030) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A sensory neuron whose soma is located in the thoracic dorsal root ganglion and that expresses endothelin-1 (EDN1). In rat DRG, EDN1 immunoreactivity has been detected in a subset of neuron somata (Giaid et al. 1989, PMID:2678100). Endothelin-1 acts as an endogenous pain mediator, sensitizing nociceptors through activation of ETA and ETB receptors, and these neurons are implicated in vascular pain signalling. EDN1-expressing DRG neurons have been characterized in rat; their prevalence and properties in human DRG remain to be systematically determined, as EDN1 is not yet represented in human DRG single-cell transcriptomic datasets." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1073/pnas.86.19.7634" 

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion endothelin-1 neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079030) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "thoracic dorsal root ganglion endothelin-1 neuron"

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion peripherin neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079033) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A small-diameter sensory neuron whose soma is located in the thoracic dorsal root ganglion and that expresses peripherin (encoded by PRPH), a type III intermediate filament protein. This neuron is unmyelinated, conducts action potentials as a C-fiber, and encompasses nociceptive and thermoceptive functional subpopulations. In human DRG, peripherin-immunoreactive neurons are predominantly of small diameter (Chang et al. 2018, PMID:28424991), distinguishing them from large-diameter myelinated A-fiber neurons that instead express neurofilament heavy chain (Haberberger et al. 2019, PMID:31293388). In mice, peripherin similarly marks unmyelinated primary afferent neurons." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1007/s12264-017-0132-3" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:31293388" 

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion peripherin neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079033) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "thoracic dorsal root ganglion peripherin neuron" 

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion peripherin neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079033) SubClassOf [BFO_0000002](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000002) 

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion peripherin neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079033) SubClassOf [sensory neuron of dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_1001451) 

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion peripherin neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079033) SubClassOf [has soma location](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002100) some [thoracic dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002835) 

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion peripherin neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079033) SubClassOf [expresses](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002292) some [peripherin](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PR_000013289)

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion substance P neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079036) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion substance P neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079036) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A peptidergic nociceptor whose soma is located in the thoracic dorsal root ganglion and that expresses substance P, an 11-amino-acid neuropeptide cleaved from protachykinin-1 (encoded by TAC1). This small-diameter neuron frequently co-expresses CGRP and belongs to the TrkA-positive peptidergic population. Upon nociceptive activation, it releases substance P both centrally in the spinal cord dorsal horn and peripherally at sensory nerve terminals, mediating neurogenic inflammation through NK1 receptor signalling (Haberberger et al. 2019, PMID:31293388). Substance P-immunoreactive neurons have been consistently identified in human DRG by immunohistochemistry across multiple studies." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "10.1016/S0304-3940(00)00772-2" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:31293388" 

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion substance P neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079036) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "thoracic dorsal root ganglion substance P neuron" 

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion substance P neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079036) SubClassOf [BFO_0000002](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000002) 

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion substance P neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079036) SubClassOf [sensory neuron of dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_1001451) 

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion substance P neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079036) SubClassOf [has soma location](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002100) some [thoracic dorsal root ganglion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002835) 

- [thoracic dorsal root ganglion substance P neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_4079036) SubClassOf [expresses](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002292) some [protachykinin-1](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PR_000016003)

- [type 1 otic fibrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0002670) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A spiral ligament fibrocyte that underlies the stria vascularis and is part of a mesenchymal gap junction network that regulates ionic homeostasis of the endolymph. In mice, expression of connexin 26 (Gjb2) and connexin 30 (Gjb6) serves as a distinguishing molecular signature."

  - [has_synonym_type](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasSynonymType) [abbreviation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0003000)

- [type 2 otic fibrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0002666) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "An otic fibrocyte that underlies the spiral prominence and is part of a mesenchymal gap junction network that regulates ionic homeostasis of the endolymph." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:18353863"

- [type 2 otic fibrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0002666) [has exact synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasExactSynonym) "type 2 spiral ligament fibrocyte" 

- [type 2 otic fibrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0002666) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A spiral ligament fibrocyte that is located near the spiral prominence between the basilar crest and the stria and plays an important role in potassium recycling by actively pumping K+ from the extracellular space and facilitating transcellular K+ transport through gap junctions toward Type I fibrocytes. In mice, Type II fibrocytes express connexin 26 (Gjb2) and connexin 30 (Gjb6) as part of the mesenchymal gap junction system, as well as the Na‑K‑2Cl cotransporter NKCC1." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:33193034" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:19080786" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:18353863" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:tfm" 

- [type 2 otic fibrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0002666) [has_related_synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasRelatedSynonym) "type II SLF" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:33193034" 

  - [has_synonym_type](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasSynonymType) [abbreviation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0003000) 

- [type 2 otic fibrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0002666) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "type II spiral ligament fibrocyte" 

- [type 2 otic fibrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0002666) SubClassOf [spiral ligament fibrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020005)

- [type 3 otic fibrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0002669) [has exact synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasExactSynonym) "type 3 spiral ligament fibrocyte" 

- [type 3 otic fibrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0002669) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A spiral ligament fibrocyte that is located in the deepest part of the inferior spiral ligament, directly lining the bony otic capsule. Distinguished from ion-transporting Type I and II fibrocytes, it utilises actin-myosin stress fibres and anchoring interactions to regulate basilar membrane tension. It has an elongated morphology, is most numerous in the basal, high-frequency cochlea, and expresses contractile and cytoskeletal proteins in mice, including &alpha;‑smooth muscle actin, non‑muscle myosin II, caldesmon, and the water channel Aquaporin‑1(Mahendrasingam et al., 2011; Kelly et al., 2012)." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:33193034" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:18353863" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:tfm" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:22476723" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:22043022" 

- [type 3 otic fibrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0002669) [has exact synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasExactSynonym) "tension fibroblast" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:33193034" 

- [type 3 otic fibrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0002669) [has_related_synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasRelatedSynonym) "type III SLF" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:33193034" 

  - [has_synonym_type](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasSynonymType) [abbreviation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0003000) 

- [type 3 otic fibrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0002669) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "type III spiral ligament fibrocyte" 

- [type 3 otic fibrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0002669) SubClassOf [spiral ligament fibrocyte](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020005)
